workflow_name: Customer Renewal Email -> Forecast Impact + Path
user_message: Just got this from Patricia Kim at Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery -- I need help figuring out deployment blockers. Board meeting is next week.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-26 09:46 EST
Subject: URGENT: deployment blockers Issue at Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery

Just checking in on the deployment blockers proposal. Our Director of IT is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
Date: 2024-02-04 15:18 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery deployment blockers

There's been a change in our timeline for deployment blockers. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-01 15:20 EST
Subject: Follow-up: deployment blockers Review

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with deployment blockers. What are the next steps from your side?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

required_artifacts: path forward with MACC/funding options if applicable

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF35-N trap (No native 'renewal motion' workflow in skill set -- current design synthesizes); produces complete Contract state summary with freshness, forecast impact with confidence, response draft (no commercia
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF35-N failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF35-N failure mode (No native 'renewal motion' workflow in skill set -- current design synthesizes)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct deployment blockers decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF35-N -- No native 'renewal motion' workflow in skill set -- current design synthesizes
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
